content.problemStatement:
Let's define the niceness of a sequence of positive integers X1,X2,‚Ä¶,XNX_1, X_2, \dots, X_NX1‚Äč,X2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,XN‚Äč as the sum of greatest common divisors of all pairs of its elements, i.e.
‚ąĎi=1N‚ąĎj=i+1Ngcd(Xi,Xj)‚ÄÖ‚Ää.\sum_{i=1}^N \sum_{j=i+1}^N \mathrm{gcd}(X_i, X_j)\;.‚ąĎi=1N‚Äč‚ąĎj=i+1N‚Äčgcd(Xi‚Äč,Xj‚Äč).

For example, the niceness of the sequence [1,2,2][1, 2, 2][1,2,2] is gcd(1,2)+gcd(1,2)+gcd(2,2)=4gcd(1, 2) + gcd(1, 2) + gcd(2, 2) = 4gcd(1,2)+gcd(1,2)+gcd(2,2)=4.

You are given a sequence A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč; each of its elements is either a positive integer or missing.

Consider all possible ways to replace each missing element of AAA by a positive integer (not necessarily the same for each element) such that the sum of all elements is equal to SSS. Your task is to find the total niceness of all resulting sequences, i.e. compute the niceness of each possible resulting sequence and sum up all these values. Since the answer may be very large, compute it modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.
constraints: 1‚Č§Ai‚Č§501 \le A_i \le 501‚Č§Ai‚Äč‚Č§50 or Ai=‚ąí1A_i = -1Ai‚Äč=‚ąí1 for each valid iii
1‚Č§Ai‚Č§181 \le A_i \le 181‚Č§Ai‚Äč‚Č§18 or Ai=‚ąí1A_i = -1Ai‚Äč=‚ąí1 for each valid iii
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and SSS.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč. Missing elements in this sequence are denoted by ‚ąí1-1‚ąí1.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚ÄĒ the total niceness modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: There is only one possible way to fill in the missing element; the resulting sequence is [1,1,1][1, 1, 1][1,1,1]. Its niceness is 333.
Example case 2: There is only three possible ways to fill in the missing elements; the resulting sequences are [1,1,3,3][1, 1, 3, 3][1,1,3,3], [1,3,1,3][1, 3, 1, 3][1,3,1,3], and [1,2,2,3][1, 2, 2, 3][1,2,2,3]. The sum of their niceness is 8+8+7=238 + 8 + 7 = 238+8+7=23.
